title: HTML Picture Element
content: ### The `` Element The `` element provides **responsive images** -- different images for different screen sizes. --- ### Basic Syntax ```html ``` --- ### How It Works 1. Browser checks each `` in order 2. Uses the **first matching** source 3. Falls back to `` if none match 4. The `` tag is **required** (for fallback and `alt` text) --- ### Attributes | Attribute | `` | `` | |-----------|-----------|---------| | `srcset` | Image URL(s) for the source | -- | | `media` | Media query condition | -- | | `sizes` | Image display sizes | -- | | `src` | -- | Fallback image | | `alt` | -- | Alternative text | --- ### Image Formats (Browser Support) ```html ``` Browsers use the first format they support. --- ### Why Use ``? - **Art direction** -- different crops for mobile vs desktop - **Format selection** -- serve WebP/AVIF to modern browsers - **Performance** -- serve smaller images on small screens > **Best Practice:** Always include a fallback `` tag. It is required for the `` element to work, and provides accessibility via `alt` text.

quiz.question: Which tag in <picture> is required as fallback?
